text: Hello friends, this is a case of thoracic sympatherectomy which we are performing for palmar hyperhidrosis.
Here on the fourth intercostal space, fourth or fifth both you can use, anterior axillary line this is optical port, this is 5 millimeter port and here we are using 5 millimeter striker

4 k telescope that is a very good quality telescope and we will use 1688 striker camera and now on the mid clavicular line this is the second intercostal space that will be used for the instrument to use energy and you can use hook there to cut the sympathetic now
here we will use the T 3 because T 2 is the first visible vertebra by the lapothoracoscopy and because only palmar hyperhidrosis is there.
So, this T 3 will be cut with the hook and this is a monopolar hook which is cutting the T 3 this is proximal end of the nerve
cutting current should be used and minimum wattage should be used so that cervicothoracic ganglion should not be damaged by remote injury.

And now laterally 4, 5 centimeter you should cut the mediastinal pleura and check for any other collateral rams or duplicate nerve that is important to find out because once you sometime collateral and duplicate nerves are there so it is better to cut it.
If periosteum will burn, patient will have little backache transiently but that will be okay within couple of days.
So now this side is over and now you can go to the opposite side.
Here we are doing the bilateral sympatherectomy so that both the side it will work and then this is the right side and these are the ribs.

And again you can find out the sympathetic nerve and then you can separate the nerve with the monopolar you can cut it and then 3 4 centimeter lateral you can separate the mediastinal pleura and you can check for any duplicate nerve on collateral rameses.
So this is very simple surgery that is thoracic sympathectomy.
Now once it is over after that in front of your telescope you should inflate the lung.

You should ask your anesthetist to give the complete tidal volume and slowly slowly telescope should be withdrawn and in front of you, you should inflate the lung so that it should not remain collapsed.

so thank you very much for watching this video this was just a simple case of thoracic sympathectomy for the palmer hyperhidrosis in a young patient patient was only 21 year old and he was suffering from severe hyperhidrosis thank you very much
